summary: Matched additional trophy/toy module functions in ty, primarily adding C bodies and prototypes for tylist and tydisplay routines, correcting signatures in toy.h and tyfigupon.h, and adding includes needed for a successful build. The PR summary explicitly claims 100% matches for un_8031B9A4_OnFrame, tyFigupon_80314BE4, and un_80305C44, plus build fixes. The diff excerpt shows new simple OnFrame wrappers, tylist cleanup/display helpers, baselib camera/display usage, and placeholder markers for still-unmatched nearby functions.
what_changed: Added matched implementation for un_8031B9A4_OnFrame in src/melee/ty/tydisplay.c: it checks M2C_FIELD(un_804A284C, s8*, 4), calls un_8031B850, then calls gm_801A4B60.
Added a very similar matched OnFrame-style function un_803182D4_OnFrame in tydisplay.c, checking the same M2C_FIELD and then calling un_803181BC and gm_801A4B60.
Expanded tydisplay.c includes to use gm_1A3F.h, gm_1A45.h, tyfigupon.h, and existing toy/tylist/textlib/random dependencies.

Populated previously near-empty tylist.c with includes, a local TyListData struct containing padding to offset 0x28 followed by HSD_CObj* cobj, externs for several un_804D6Exx globals, matched body for un_803127D4, and matched body for fn_80314504.
Implemented un_803127D4 to clear a sequence of tylist global pointers to NULL and memzero un_804A2AA8 for 0x14 bytes.

Implemented fn_80314504 as a camera/display callback: casts the GObj to TyListData, sets the current camera, erases the screen, runs HSD_GObj_80390ED0 with link/order 7, disables fog, and ends the current camera.
Added tylist.h declarations for fn_80314504 and included baselib/forward.h so HSD_GObj can be referenced in the prototype.

Updated toy.h signatures from broad placeholders to more accurate matched forms: Toy_RemoveUserData now names its void* parameter ptr, un_80306D14 now returns void and takes void, and un_803102D0 now returns void and takes void.
Updated tyfigupon.h signature for tyFigupon_80314BE4 to name parameters as HSD_GObj* gobj and int unused, matching the claimed 100% function match.
Added missing includes in toy.c for gm/gm_1A45.h, lb/lbarchive.h, and lb/lbaudio_ax.h.
Added an asm placeholder comment marker for fn_8031EFE4 in src/melee/vi/vi0801.c without a shown implementation change.
Left numerous neighboring functions as decomp placeholder comments such as /// #un_803181BC, /// #un_803124BC, and /// #fn_8031438C, preserving map context around newly matched routines.
smart_moves: Kept the PR narrowly focused on a second tranche of trophy/toy function matches while also fixing only the headers and includes required for the new matches to build.
Converted generic UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes to concrete void(void) signatures once the functions were understood, improving type safety and reducing future matching ambiguity.

Used a minimal local struct layout for TyListData with explicit padding to reach the observed HSD_CObj* field at offset 0x28, avoiding overclaiming unknown fields while enabling a matched display callback.

Preserved unmatched surrounding functions as address-comment placeholders, which helps future decomp work maintain file ordering and identify remaining gaps without pretending those functions are understood.
Named an apparently unused parameter as unused in tyFigupon_80314BE4, documenting intent while keeping the ABI/signature required for matching.
Added the exact baselib and module headers needed by the new C bodies instead of relying on implicit declarations.
decomp_lessons: For small OnFrame functions, exact matches can often be achieved by expressing the original guard as an M2C_FIELD access to an unresolved global structure field, followed by the original call sequence.

When only one field of a structure is needed for a match, a temporary local struct with padding plus the known field can be an acceptable incremental tactic, especially if the unknown fields are not yet named.
Header cleanup should accompany function matching: replacing UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS with concrete return and parameter types prevents later mismatches and compiler ambiguity.

A matched implementation may require adding missing transitive dependencies explicitly, as seen with gm_1A45, lbarchive, lbaudio_ax, baselib/cobj, displayfunc, fog, gobj, gobjgxlink, and video headers.
Global reset functions may match cleanly as direct NULL assignments plus a memzero of a known global byte array; avoid inventing semantic names for globals until their purpose is understood.
Use address-bearing placeholder comments to keep decompilation progress searchable and to mark functions that remain for future work.
naming_conventions: Matched but still-unknown functions retained address-based names such as un_8031B9A4_OnFrame, un_803182D4_OnFrame, un_803127D4, and fn_80314504.
On-frame callbacks use the suffix _OnFrame, consistent with un_8031B9A4_OnFrame and un_803182D4_OnFrame.
Known module-prefixed functions use camel/module prefixes such as Toy_RemoveUserData, tyFigupon_80314BE4, and tyDisplay_8031C2CC.
Unused parameters may be explicitly named unused in headers, as with tyFigupon_80314BE4(HSD_GObj* gobj, int unused).
Partially understood local data types can use module-specific provisional names such as TyListData while leaving fields padded until understood.
Address comments in headers continue to use the /* 314BE4 */ style before declarations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used M2C_FIELD(un_804A284C, s8*, 4) to reproduce access to an unresolved global/struct byte or pointer field at offset 4.
Used explicit padding in TyListData, u8 pad[0x28], so the cobj field lands at the required offset for codegen.
Casted HSD_GObj* directly to TyListData* in fn_80314504, suggesting the original object/userdata layout or callback argument layout required that exact access pattern.
Matched display code by preserving the call order: HSD_CObjSetCurrent, HSD_SetEraseColor, HSD_CObjEraseScreen, HSD_GObj_80390ED0, HSD_FogSet, HSD_CObjEndCurrent.
Matched reset code with explicit ordered assignments to multiple un_804D6Exx globals followed by memzero(un_804A2AA8, 0x14).
Used concrete void(void) prototypes for functions that take no arguments to prevent old-style or placeholder parameter handling from affecting codegen.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.lesson: Do not commit local agent/build validation artifacts with decomp changes.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.comment: some garbage from my agent scaffolding that i now have commit hooks to prevent from getting committed again :) will remove.
review_feedback.lesson: Use commit hooks or similar safeguards to prevent generated scaffolding files from entering PRs.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or fully define TyListData and the un_804D6Exx globals used in tylist.c.
Search for subsequent matches of the placeholder functions around tyDisplay, including un_803181BC, un_8031830C, un_8031B850, and un_8031C1D0.
Check whether un_804A284C later receives a real struct type replacing M2C_FIELD accesses.
Find the companion part 1 PR for trophy/toy module functions to understand the broader matching sequence.
Search for the implementation body of tyFigupon_80314BE4 to see the exact match that motivated the header parameter names.
Verify that .build_validated was removed from the final merged diff or a follow-up cleanup.
